channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: North Carolina justices block certification of election outcome in race for one of its own seats
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina's highest court blocked on Tuesday the certification of a November election result for one of its own seats so it can review legal arguments by a trailing candidate who contends over 60,000 ballots that were cast shouldn't be counted.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Woman jumps from car sliding on ice in Kansas City
item.description: When one driver in Kansas City hit a patch of black ice, her first instinct was to jump from her vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Feds release new photo of New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar as FBI seeks more tips
item.description: FBI releases image showing New Orleans terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar biking through the French Quarter on Halloween, months before attacking New Year's revelers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to stay safe during Southern California’s latest Santa Ana wind event
item.description: Whether you live in these communities or adjacent, officials are urging residents to take precautions to stay safe during the latest wind event.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's Mar-a-Lago press conference
item.description: PALM BEACH, Florida (AP) — Less than two weeks before taking office, President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday took some of his most audacious claims and promises of the transition period and amped them up to new levels.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, AAMER MADHANI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he will change the name of the Gulf of Mexico
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said Tuesday that he would move to try to rename the Gulf of Mexico to the “Gulf of America,” a name he said has a “beautiful ring to it.” It's his latest suggestion to redraw the map of the Western Hemisphere.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Payments go out in $100M Verizon settlement, but people are disappointed
item.description: Payments have finally started going out in the $100 million class action lawsuit Verizon agreed to settle, but the deposits are falling short of people's expectations.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Man accused of setting woman on fire on Brooklyn subway pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta has been accused of intentionally setting fire to 57-year-old Debrina Kawam of New Jersey at an NYC subway station on Dec. 22.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Peter Yarrow of Peter, Paul and Mary dies at 86
item.description: Yarrow, who also co-wrote the group's most enduring song, “Puff the Magic Dragon,” died Tuesday in New York.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KRON4

item.title: New Orleans attack came at a time of political heat, and transition, for the FBI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Joining law enforcement officials to update the public in the hours after a man killed 14 people and injured dozens of others in New Orleans, Louisiana's junior Republican senator, John Kennedy, implored the FBI to “catch these people" — and then added one more eyebrow-raising requ
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illegal charged with lighting sleeping woman on fire pleads not guilty
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta, the Guatemalan man accused of lighting a sleeping subway rider on fire and watching her burn to death on a Brooklyn subway car, was arraigned Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump delivers first remarks as certified election winner
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump gave remarks on Tuesday morning focused on his economic agenda from his Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tibet earthquake kills at least 126 people and leaves many trapped
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — A strong earthquake shook a high-altitude region of western China and areas of Nepal on Tuesday, damaging hundreds of houses, littering streets with rubble and killing at least 126 people in Tibet. Many others were trapped as dozens of aftershocks shook the remote region.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 2 bodies found in landing gear of JetBlue plane
item.description: Authorities say two bodies have been found in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue aircraft at a South Florida airport.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jimmy Carter's casket arrives at the US Capitol, where he will lie in state
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis to overhaul police training, use-of-force policies in wake of George Floyd's murder
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council approved a deal to overhaul the city's police training and use-of-force policies over the 2020 killing of George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's sentencing still on for Friday after judge rebuffs his push for a delay while he appeals
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump remains on track to be sentenced this week in his hush money case after a judge on Monday denied his request to halt proceedings while he appeals a ruling that upheld the historic verdict. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zuckerberg taps UFC President Dana White for Meta's board of directors
item.description: (KRON) -- Meta founder and CEO Mark Zuckerberg rings in the new year with several major personnel announcements. Three high-profile individuals have been named to join Meta's board of directors, including one of the most prominent figures in sports.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: The drama-free proceeding stood in stark contrast to four years ago when pro-Trump rioters violently stormed the Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star allegedly choked, punched children's mother: police
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by Nexstar's WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, ABC 4

item.title: Biden issues ban on offshore oil and gas drilling in most federal waters. Trump vows to undo it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is moving to ban new offshore oil and gas drilling in most U.S. coastal waters, a last-minute effort to block possible action by the incoming Trump administration to expand offshore drilling.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Winter weather can disrupt. Here's what to do if your flight is canceled
item.description: Winter weather often causes flight delays and cancellations. While airlines can’t control the weather, they are required in the U.S. to provide refunds to customers whose flights are canceled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lawsuit alleges ex-Fox Sports host Skip Bayless harassed hairstylist, offered her $1.5 million for sex
item.description: The complaint claims Fox executives fostered a hostile work environment that allowed senior managers and on-air personalities including Bayless to abuse workers without fear of punishment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Detachment risk prompts recall of 145K trailer hitch locks
item.description: The recall says the vehicle may become detached from whatever it's towing due to the potentially loose part on the hitch locks.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: The Latest: The Golden Globes kick off award season
item.description: Hollywood got dressed up as the Golden Globes returned for its annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television workers at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, California. The show serves as the ceremonial start to the 2025 awards season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patriots fire coach Jerod Mayo shortly after beating Bills to finish his lone season at 4-13
item.description: FOXBOROUGH, Mass. (AP) — The New England Patriots fired coach Jerod Mayo on Sunday after one season, beginning another reboot for the franchise just a year after it parted with longtime coach Bill Belichick.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The heaviest snowfall in a decade is possible as a wintry blast roils parts of the US
item.description: A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday, as a disruptive winter storm brought the possibility of the heaviest snowfall in a decade to some areas.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — At a Mass celebrated Sunday at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, just blocks from where a man sped down Bourbon Street in a deadly attack early on New Year's Day, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains and asked for prayers for victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A soccer-loving nun from Brazil tops list of world's oldest living person at nearly 117
item.description: A soccer-loving nun from Brazil is believed to have become the world's oldest living person at nearly 117 following the recent death of a woman from Japan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The FBI says the man responsible for New Year's truck attack visited New Orleans twice before
item.description: Shamsud-Din Jabbar, a U.S. citizen from Houston, also traveled to Cairo, Egypt, as well as Ontario, Canada, before the attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, ABC 4

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, ABC 4

item.title: Selena's killer, Yolanda Saldívar, files for parole, begins review process
item.description: Saldívar, now 64 years-old, was sentenced to life in prison on Oct. 26, 1995, with the possibility of parole after 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Mendez, WGN 9
